items.area: Economic buyer name and direct engagement
items.quote: They are meeting CFO week of 13th July to decided on scope.

sources: Avoma RFP briefing, 12 Jan 2026 (Duncan Tennyson, Tej Ram)
metrics.narrative: The evaluated scope is quantified in size (ANZ indirect spend of roughly US$110M via POs and US$130M non-PO, per the 12 Jan 2026 RFP briefing, 10 procurement power users and ~200 business-user contributors) but no formal ROI or savings business case has been built. The current driving 'metric' is risk-avoidance: TTI wants a phased, lower-risk rollout because of its SAP ECC6-to-S/4HANA migration, and is asking Zycus for a proper RACI and project plan 'like all the other vendors gave them' (Next Step, 1 Jul 2026) before it will commit to scope.

champion.narrative: Tej Ram (Manager, Procurement & Transformation) is the closest thing to a champion: he has been present at nearly every touchpoint since Jan 2025, replied directly to Carl Kimball (the deal owner's manager) on 30 Jun 2026 to confirm references were complete, and is jointly driving the S2C pivot with Duncan Tennyson. However, there is no evidence he has advocated for Zycus with the CFO, and his and Duncan's roles are both evaluator/technical, not sponsor-level. Champion strength is developing, not confirmed.

competition.narrative: Coupa, GEP, iValua and SAP Ariba were named as the field on 13 Jan 2026 ('Good position - Zycus seen as strong solution... vs Coupa/iValua/Ariba'). By 11 May 2026, GEP and iValua were explicitly flagged as 'strong threats' (SAP Ariba no longer mentioned, suggesting it faded from the shortlist). On 1 Jul 2026, Duncan and Tej indicated the emerging S2C-only scope 'puts Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa,' but also noted GEP, iValua and Coupa had already delivered a RACI and phased implementation plan that Zycus has not yet matched.

sources: Avoma demo call, 2 Jul 2025 (buyer speaker, pain_point note)
identify_pain.narrative: The core pain is de-risking a major ERP transition: TTI is moving from the relevant stakeholder6 to S/4HANA and wants a vendor selection that doesn't add integration risk, hence the pivot toward a smaller, phased S2C-only scope with a properly documented RACI and project plan (Next_Step_History__c, 1 Jul 2026). Supporting pain was also named directly on the 2 Jul 2025 demo call: 'Managing a single budget for a project that involves multiple vendors and purchase requests presents challenges in tracking budget utilization and monitoring potential overutilization' (buyer speaker, Zycus demo, 2 Jul 2025).

sources: Next_Step_History__c (full history reviewed, no paper-process entries found)
paper_process.narrative: No MSA, SOW, or contracting activity has started. The deal is still pre-scope-decision (S2C vs S2P), and Next_Step_History__c contains no mention of legal review, redlines, or signature planning. This is expected at Shortlisted, but it means there is no forcing function yet to support a 30 Jul 2026 close.

sources: Avoma call 'quick catch up - TTI', 12 Dec 2025 (speaker attributed to TTI side)
economic_buyer.narrative: The CFO (Melbourne-based) is the named economic buyer across the deal's history: he mandated the tender before year-end (Avoma call, 12 Dec 2025) and now owns the final scope-and-vendor decision, meeting internally the week of 13 Jul 2026 (Next Step, 1 Jul 2026). Zycus has never had direct contact with this person; every touch runs through Tej Ram and Duncan Tennyson, both technical evaluators. No Salesforce contact role exists for the CFO. This is the single biggest structural risk on the deal right now.

decision_process.narrative: The process is explicit and has been tracked end to end: RFP issued via the relevant stakeholder, written responses, demos, shortlist to 2 vendors (11 May 2026), sandbox/reference validation, and a final CFO decision on scope and vendor (week of 13 Jul 2026). Duncan Tennyson is the named process owner ('I am project managing this RFP', RFP briefing, 12 Jan 2026), with the CFO as final approver.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented: a 350-question RFP questionnaire submitted via the relevant stakeholder (12 Feb 2026), a 3-hour onsite demo (10 Mar 2026), reference checks (Nissan confirmed 2 Jun 2026, TataPlay confirmed by 30 Jun 2026), and now a requirement for a formal RACI and phased implementation/costing plan matching competitor submissions (1 Jul 2026). SAP ECC6 integration now, with a defined migration path to S/4HANA, is an explicit technical criterion (RFP briefing, 12 Jan 2026).

items.name: Zycus sends updated commercial offer
items.summary: Luke Dougherty (Zycus) delivered the reworked Source-to-Contract commercial proposal to Tej and Duncan, built out with Carl based on TTI's stated requirements, and floated adding a Spend Analytics option for Phase One as an extra angle for consideration; he's offering to meet in person in Melbourne this week or discuss by phone, so a reply from TTI is now pending.

day_summary.overall: Zycus sent over the revised Source-to-Contract commercial offer that Luke and Carl had been building out, closing the loop on TTI's requirements list and folding in a Spend Analytics option the solutions team flagged as valuable for Phase One. Luke's proactively offering to walk Tej and Duncan through it in person while he's in Melbourne this week — the ball is now in TTI's court to review and decide whether an in-person or phone follow-up is warranted.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A genuinely live, well-tracked evaluation with real recent buyer engagement and a scope shift that currently favors Zycus over one rival, but capped by an unengaged economic buyer and an unmatched competitive submission just days before the decisive scope-and-vendor call.

deal_risk.text: A scope pivot to S2C-only would shrink the deal from $220,000 (full S2P, 8 products) to an estimated $110,000-$130,000, deferring P2P and Merlin Intake revenue to 2027.
The CFO's decision (week of 13 Jul 2026) precedes the recorded close date (30 Jul 2026) by only about two weeks, with no contracting activity yet underway.

win_position.text: On 1 Jul 2026 the buyer's project manager and procurement lead told Zycus the scope is likely narrowing to S2C-only, which they said puts Zycus in a stronger position against Coupa.
Reference checks (Nissan, confirmed 2 Jun 2026; TataPlay, completed by 30 Jun 2026) have cleared, a gating step the buyer required before further technical evaluation.
The economic buyer (the CFO, Melbourne-based) has never had direct contact with Zycus and will decide scope and vendor the week of 13 Jul 2026 without a Zycus-delivered pricing/ROI conversation.
GEP and iValua, named 'strong threats' on 11 May 2026, already delivered a RACI and phased implementation plan that Zycus only began sending on 8 Jul 2026.

deal_momentum.text: Buyer-initiated commercial engagement is fresh: Duncan Tennyson and Tej Ram asked for implementation-option and phased-costing discussion on 30 Jun 2026, and Zycus delivered revised pricing on 8 Jul 2026.

The close date has already been pushed once (15 Jun 2026 note: 'unlikely to get awarded and signed in <2 weeks') and the CFO's own scope decision (week of 13 Jul 2026) leaves very little runway to the current 30 Jul 2026 close date.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer proactively reached out on 1 Jul 2026 to redirect the deal toward S2C scope rather than letting it lapse, and completed both requested references by 30 Jun 2026.
Commitment is expressed entirely through the evaluator team (Tej Ram, Duncan Tennyson); no buyer-side executive has personally engaged Zycus.

hard.ais_why:
• Per the 11 May 2026 rep note: 'confirmed by email- shortlisted to 2 for further implementation plans, commercials and hands on sandbox- need urgent and solid local reference. GEP, iValua strong threats here.' Across 3,282 chars of NextStep spanning Jan 2025 to May 2026 ‚Äî covering RFP, demos, onsite presentations and shortlisting ‚Äî zero mention of AI, Merlin, agentic, or autonomous despite Merlin, Merlin Intake, and Agentic AI being in product scope.
• On 5 Mar 2026: 'Onsite presentation/demo for 3 hours scheduled for 10th March.' On 12 Mar 2026: 'Completed onsite demo, they have a few more vendors to review.' On 12 Dec 2025: 'CFO requested to initiate RFP around 5th January, looking to decide on a vendor by April and start implementation.' The decision framework is CFO-led, vendor-comparison-driven (vs GEP/iValua/Coupa/Ariba) with no AI-specific evaluation criterion captured.
• Confidence is Medium: heavy AI product loadout (3 AI SKUs) signals Zycus positioning, but the buyer-side dialogue captured across 16 months is pure traditional S2P RFP ‚Äî onsite demos, BAFO, 'hands on sandbox', local reference. The 4/10 score reflects AI in scope without AI in conversation; would move up only if hands-on sandbox produces AI-specific buyer engagement.
